---
title: "SO_P010: Config-Driven Customization Principle"
subtitle: "All company-specific content must be derived from configuration, not hardcoded"
chapter: "CH01"
category: "principle"
number: "SO_P010"
date-created: "2025-04-02"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "principle"
law: "Structure & Organization Law"
article: "Article 10"
derives_from:
  - "CH00.MP017: Separation of Concerns"
  - "CH00.MP011: Sensible Defaults"
related_to:
  - "DM_P001: Data Integrity"
  - "SO_P007: App.R is Global"
  - "SO_R005: App YAML Configuration"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article Overview {#overview}

**Structure & Organization Law Article 10 (Config-Driven Customization Principle)**

This article establishes that in application mode, all company-specific content, options, and behavior must be derived from `app_config` or initialization parameters, never hardcoded in the application code. Any element that varies by company must be configurable without code modification.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Principle
Application code must be company-agnostic. All company-specific elements including UI text, business logic parameters, and feature flags must be externalized to configuration files, enabling the same codebase to serve multiple companies without modification.
:::

# Section 1: Rationale {#rationale}

## Subsection 1: Benefits of Configuration-Driven Design {#benefits}

This principle ensures critical architectural benefits:

1. **Reusability**: Application code can be used across different companies without modification
2. **Maintainability**: Changes to company-specific elements are made in configuration, not code
3. **Separation of Concerns**: Code handles logic while configuration provides context-specific parameters
4. **Scalability**: New companies can be onboarded without code changes
5. **Testability**: Easier to test with different configuration scenarios

## Subsection 2: Problems Avoided {#problems-avoided}

第一項：硬編碼問題 (Hardcoding problems)

Without config-driven customization:
- Code becomes company-specific and non-reusable
- Changes require developer intervention
- Multiple code branches for different companies
- Testing complexity increases exponentially
- Deployment becomes error-prone

第二項：維護負擔 (Maintenance burden)

Hardcoded values create:
- Technical debt accumulation
- Increased bug surface area
- Difficult onboarding process
- Inconsistent implementations
- Version control conflicts

# Section 2: Configuration Sources Hierarchy {#hierarchy}

## Subsection 1: Priority Order {#priority}

Company-specific content should be sourced from (in order of precedence):

第一項：主要配置來源 (Primary configuration sources)

```r
# 1. app_config loaded during initialization (highest priority)
config <- yaml::read_yaml("app_config.yaml")
company_name <- config$company$name

# 2. Database tables with company-specific parameters
company_params <- tbl(con, "company_parameters") %>%
  filter(company_id == current_company) %>%
  collect()

# 3. User preferences stored in application state
user_prefs <- reactiveValues(
  theme = "default",
  language = "en"
)

# 4. Environment variables (only for API keys, credentials)
api_key <- Sys.getenv("COMPANY_API_KEY")
```

## Subsection 2: Configuration Structure {#structure}

第一項：標準配置結構 (Standard configuration structure)

```yaml
# app_config.yaml
company:
  name: "ACME Corporation"
  logo: "assets/acme_logo.png"
  theme:
    primary_color: "#007bff"
    secondary_color: "#6c757d"
  
  # UI Configuration
  marketing_channels:
    "Amazon": "amazon"
    "Official Website": "website"
    "Retail Store": "retail"
  
  default_channel: "amazon"
  
  # Business Logic Configuration
  discount_thresholds: [100, 500, 1000]
  discount_rates: [0.05, 0.10, 0.15]
  
  # Feature Flags
  features:
    advanced_analytics: true
    ai_recommendations: false
    export_functionality: true
```

# Section 3: What Must Be Configurable {#configurable}

## Subsection 1: UI Elements {#ui-elements}

第一項：視覺元素 (Visual elements)

```r
# Company branding
ui_header <- dashboardHeader(
  title = config$company$name,  # Not hardcoded
  titleWidth = config$ui$header_width
)

# Theme colors
tags$style(HTML(sprintf("
  .main-header .logo {
    background-color: %s !important;
  }
  .btn-primary {
    background-color: %s !important;
    border-color: %s !important;
  }
", config$company$theme$primary_color,
   config$company$theme$primary_color,
   config$company$theme$primary_color)))
```

第二項：互動元素 (Interactive elements)

```r
# Dropdown options from config
selectInput(
  inputId = "channel",
  label = translate("Select Channel"),
  choices = config$company$marketing_channels,  # Config-driven
  selected = config$company$default_channel
)

# Radio buttons from config
radioButtons(
  inputId = "analysis_type",
  label = translate("Analysis Type"),
  choices = config$analysis$available_types,  # Not hardcoded
  selected = config$analysis$default_type
)
```

第三項：導航結構 (Navigation structure)

```r
# Menu items from configuration
create_menu <- function(config) {
  menu_items <- lapply(config$navigation$items, function(item) {
    if (item$enabled) {  # Feature flag check
      menuItem(
        text = translate(item$label),
        tabName = item$id,
        icon = icon(item$icon)
      )
    }
  })
  
  do.call(sidebarMenu, compact(menu_items))
}
```

## Subsection 2: Business Logic {#business-logic}

第一項：計算規則 (Calculation rules)

```r
# Company-specific calculation rules
calculate_metrics <- function(data, config) {
  # Use config-driven thresholds
  data %>%
    mutate(
      risk_level = case_when(
        value < config$thresholds$low ~ "Low",
        value < config$thresholds$medium ~ "Medium",
        value < config$thresholds$high ~ "High",
        TRUE ~ "Critical"
      ),
      discount = calculate_discount(value, config$discount_rules)
    )
}

# Discount calculation using config
calculate_discount <- function(order_value, rules) {
  applicable_rate <- 0
  
  for (i in seq_along(rules$thresholds)) {
    if (order_value >= rules$thresholds[i]) {
      applicable_rate <- rules$rates[i]
    }
  }
  
  return(order_value * applicable_rate)
}
```

第二項：驗證規則 (Validation rules)

```r
# Config-driven validation
validate_input <- function(value, config) {
  rules <- config$validation$rules
  
  # Check minimum
  if (!is.null(rules$min) && value < rules$min) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = sprintf("Value must be at least %s", rules$min)
    ))
  }
  
  # Check maximum
  if (!is.null(rules$max) && value > rules$max) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = sprintf("Value must not exceed %s", rules$max)
    ))
  }
  
  # Check pattern
  if (!is.null(rules$pattern) && !grepl(rules$pattern, value)) {
    return(list(
      valid = FALSE,
      message = rules$pattern_message
    ))
  }
  
  return(list(valid = TRUE))
}
```

## Subsection 3: Feature Availability {#features}

第一項：功能旗標 (Feature flags)

```r
# Conditional feature rendering
render_features <- function(config) {
  ui_elements <- list()
  
  # Advanced analytics feature
  if (config$features$advanced_analytics) {
    ui_elements$analytics <- analyticsModuleUI("analytics")
  }
  
  # AI recommendations feature
  if (config$features$ai_recommendations) {
    ui_elements$ai <- aiRecommendationsUI("ai")
  }
  
  # Export functionality
  if (config$features$export_functionality) {
    ui_elements$export <- downloadButton(
      "export_data",
      translate("Export Data")
    )
  }
  
  return(ui_elements)
}
```

# Section 4: Implementation Patterns {#patterns}

## Subsection 1: Correct Implementation {#correct}

第一項：配置驅動實作 (Config-driven implementation)

```r
# CORRECT - All company-specific content from config
create_dashboard <- function(config) {
  # UI components use config
  sidebar_items <- lapply(config$sidebar$sections, function(section) {
    if (section$type == "radio") {
      prettyRadioButtons(
        inputId = ns(section$id),
        label = translate(section$label),
        choices = section$choices,  # From config
        selected = section$default   # From config
      )
    } else if (section$type == "select") {
      selectInput(
        inputId = ns(section$id),
        label = translate(section$label),
        choices = section$choices,  # From config
        selected = section$default   # From config
      )
    }
  })
  
  return(sidebar_items)
}
```

## Subsection 2: Incorrect Implementation {#incorrect}

第一項：硬編碼錯誤 (Hardcoding errors)

```r
# INCORRECT - Hardcoded company-specific options
create_channel_selector <- function() {
  # Don't hardcode company-specific choices
  radioButtons(
    inputId = "channel",
    label = "Marketing Channel",
    choices = list(
      "Amazon" = "amazon",           # Hardcoded!
      "Official Website" = "website"  # Hardcoded!
    ),
    selected = "amazon"  # Hardcoded default!
  )
}

# INCORRECT - Hardcoded business logic
calculate_discount <- function(value) {
  # Don't hardcode company-specific thresholds
  if (value > 1000) {      # Hardcoded threshold!
    return(value * 0.15)   # Hardcoded rate!
  } else if (value > 500) { # Hardcoded threshold!
    return(value * 0.10)   # Hardcoded rate!
  } else {
    return(value * 0.05)   # Hardcoded rate!
  }
}
```

# Section 5: Exception Handling {#exceptions}

## Subsection 1: Missing Configuration {#missing-config}

第一項：優雅降級 (Graceful degradation)

```r
# Handle missing configuration gracefully
get_config_value <- function(config, path, default = NULL) {
  # Split path into components
  keys <- strsplit(path, "\\.")[[1]]
  
  # Navigate through config
  value <- config
  for (key in keys) {
    if (is.null(value) || !key %in% names(value)) {
      # Log warning about missing config
      warning(sprintf(
        "Configuration missing: %s, using default: %s",
        path, 
        deparse(default)
      ))
      return(default)
    }
    value <- value[[key]]
  }
  
  return(value)
}

# Usage with fallback
channels <- get_config_value(
  config,
  "company.marketing_channels",
  default = list("Default" = "default")
)
```

第二項：配置驗證 (Configuration validation)

```r
# Validate configuration on startup
validate_config <- function(config) {
  required_fields <- c(
    "company.name",
    "company.marketing_channels",
    "company.default_channel"
  )
  
  missing <- character()
  
  for (field in required_fields) {
    if (is.null(get_config_value(config, field))) {
      missing <- c(missing, field)
    }
  }
  
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Required configuration missing: %s\nPlease update app_config.yaml",
      paste(missing, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  
  return(TRUE)
}
```

# Section 6: Best Practices {#best-practices}

## Subsection 1: Configuration Documentation {#documentation}

```yaml
# Document configuration structure
# config_template.yaml
company:
  name: string                    # Required: Company display name
  logo: string                    # Optional: Path to logo file
  
  marketing_channels:             # Required: Available channels
    # Format: "Display Name": "internal_id"
    # At least one channel required
  
  default_channel: string         # Required: Must match a channel ID
  
  features:                       # Optional: Feature flags
    advanced_analytics: boolean   # Default: false
    ai_recommendations: boolean   # Default: false
  
  # Business Rules
  discount_thresholds: [number]  # Required: Ascending order
  discount_rates: [number]       # Required: Same length as thresholds
```

## Subsection 2: Testing Strategies {#testing}

```r
# Test with multiple configurations
test_that("app works with different configs", {
  # Test config 1: Basic company
  config1 <- list(
    company = list(
      name = "Test Company 1",
      marketing_channels = list("Web" = "web"),
      default_channel = "web"
    )
  )
  
  app1 <- create_app(config1)
  expect_true(validate_app(app1))
  
  # Test config 2: Advanced company
  config2 <- list(
    company = list(
      name = "Test Company 2",
      marketing_channels = list(
        "Amazon" = "amazon",
        "eBay" = "ebay",
        "Website" = "web"
      ),
      default_channel = "amazon",
      features = list(
        advanced_analytics = TRUE,
        ai_recommendations = TRUE
      )
    )
  )
  
  app2 <- create_app(config2)
  expect_true(validate_app(app2))
  expect_true(has_feature(app2, "advanced_analytics"))
})
```

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## Config-Driven Customization Checklist

**Configuration Structure:**
- [ ] app_config.yaml contains all company-specific values
- [ ] Configuration schema is documented
- [ ] Required vs optional fields are clear
- [ ] Default values are specified

**UI Elements:**
- [ ] Company name and branding from config
- [ ] All dropdown/radio options from config
- [ ] Menu structure from config
- [ ] Theme colors from config

**Business Logic:**
- [ ] Calculation rules from config
- [ ] Validation thresholds from config
- [ ] Default values from config
- [ ] Feature flags control availability

**Code Quality:**
- [ ] No hardcoded company names
- [ ] No hardcoded business rules
- [ ] No hardcoded UI options
- [ ] Graceful handling of missing config

**Testing:**
- [ ] Tests use multiple configurations
- [ ] Missing config handling tested
- [ ] Feature flags tested
- [ ] Configuration validation tested
:::

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The Config-Driven Customization Principle ensures that applications remain company-agnostic and highly maintainable by externalizing all company-specific elements to configuration files. This approach enables a single codebase to serve multiple companies, simplifies onboarding, and reduces maintenance burden while improving testability and scalability.

---

*Based on: Structure & Organization Law Article 10*  
*Derives from: [MP017 Separation of Concerns](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP017_separation_of_concerns.qmd), [MP011 Sensible Defaults](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/MP011_sensible_defaults.qmd)*  
*Related to: [SO_P007 App.R is Global](SO_P007_app_r_is_global.qmd), [SO_R005 App YAML Configuration](../rules/SO_R005_app_yaml_configuration.qmd)*
